test_focus: User opens the room with a pure greeting and no business signal. Validates Ideation as the first voice (not CX), orchestrator honoring the soft-signal rule on turn 1, and Ideation stepping back once the user names any direction in R2.

profile.name: Jonathan
profile.business_type: new business, just created — type not yet disclosed on turn 1

challenge: Pre-business opener — has a new entity but has not named the work yet. Wants orientation before advice.
personality: brief, polite, willing to share once welcomed
backstory: Jonathan just formed a Connecticut LLC for a business he's starting and is testing whether the room is worth bringing his actual problem to. His first message is a greeting only. If welcomed warmly and asked what he's here to work on, he'll disclose on R2 that it's a new business in CT and he wants to talk through how to get it off the ground.
conversational_behaviors: Opens with only a greeting, no business context
Volunteers his name and general situation on R2 if the room feels inviting
Not looking for advice on R1 — looking for a reason to keep talking
Uses plain language, no business vocabulary

expected_answer_style.q2_if_welcomed: Hi, I'm Jonathan and I want to talk about my new business I just created, I'm setting it up in CT.

_note_multi_round: Single-objective persona (Protocol A adjacent). R1 tests Ideation routing; R2 tests handoff. r3/r4 null — orchestrator should have moved off Ideation by then and the conversation proceeds normally with whichever specialist picks up Jonathan's new-business question.

expected_routing.r2: NOT ideation (user has named a direction — a new CT business) — any specialist, most likely Marketer or Realist for a new-business orientation conversation
